channel.title: Finance
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Finance, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: NYT Strands Hints, Spangram, Answers: Monday, February 23 (Strike A Chord)
item.description: Looking for some help with today's NYT Strands? An extra hint and the answers are right here to help you finish the grid and keep your streak intact.

item.title: Beware Of Hotel Coffee Makers. Travel TikToker Washes Underwear There
item.description: A TikToker shared a travel hack that has gone viral and involves cleaning her underwear in a hotel coffee maker. Here's what to do with a coffee machi...

item.title: Apple’s New Low Cost MacBook Is Shaped By Walmart’s Experiment
item.description: The blueprint for Apple’s new low-cost MacBook A18 Pro wasn’t born in a lab in Cupertino. Instead, it was defined in the aisles of an American retail ...

item.title: TSA says PreCheck is still operational, right after DHS head Kristi Noem said it was suspended due to government shutdown
item.description: It was not immediately clear whether Global Entry, another airport service, would be affected.

item.title: Top EU lawmaker proposes pause on U.S. trade deal ratification, citing ‘pure tariff chaos’ from Trump administration
item.description: “No one can make sense of it anymore — only open questions and growing uncertainty for the EU and other U.S. trading partners.”

item.title: A two-child household must earn $400,000 a year for childcare to be affordable, study says. ‘It’s easy to see why birth rates are falling’
item.description: “Most parents could tell you that child care costs are astronomical these days and can cause a major financial burden, even for high-income families."

item.title: Older adults are heading back to school and represent the ‘new majority student’ as they seek up-skilling or a career change
item.description: “I think this is tied to access. Many of our learners, not all of them, haven’t imagined themselves in any kind of higher education, post-secondary ed...

item.title: Hoping for a tariff refund? Trump officials say they are waiting for lower courts to provide instructions. ‘They created the situation’
item.description: "I'm not going to get out ahead of the court. We will follow the court's direction. But as I said that could be weeks or months away.

item.title: NYT Connections Today: Answers Explained For Monday, February 23 (#988)
item.description: Not sure what today's NYT Connections answers are all about? Find out just what the different words in today's grid mean and how they fit together.

item.title: Quordle Hints And Answers For Monday, February 23
item.description: Looking for some help with today’s Quordle words? Some hints and the answers are right here to give you a hand.

item.title: Gold sheds its safe-haven status. Is it just another momentum play now?
Supreme Court tariff ruling has economists even more stressed over the U.S. debt
Could these 6 non-AI chip stocks be the next leg of the AI boom?
Trump raises global tariff to 15% in latest response to Supreme Court ruling
Asset managers' stocks fell on private-credit fears. What’s worrying investors.
US economy grew slower than expected in fourth quarter
item.description: The U.S. economy grew at a rate of 1.4% in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to an estimate released on Friday by the Bureau of Economic Analysis.

item.title: Deadliest jobs in America revealed
item.description: The United States Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that there were more than 5,000 deadly work injuries recorded in the nation in 2024.

item.title: Will refunds be issued after Supreme Court ruling on Trump tariffs?
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's IEEPA tariffs, which could open the door to billions of dollars in tariff refunds, though the r...

item.title: How should businesses approach tariff refunds?
item.description: Trade experts note that businesses seeking tariff refunds may need to appeal to CBP or the Court of International Trade to get their money back after ...

item.title: Fed's favored inflation gauge showed consumer price growth remained elevated in December
item.description: The Commerce Department released the PCE inflation report on Friday, which showed the Fed's favored inflation gauge remained elevated as consumers dea...

item.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
item.description: DeepSeek, SoFi and more in the latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom.

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Raving About a Made-in-China AI Model
item.description: DeepSeek is called “amazing and impressive” despite working with less-advanced chips.

item.title: What to Know About China's DeepSeek AI
item.description: The Chinese upstart says it has trained high-performing AI models cheaply, without using the most advanced chips.

item.title: Reid Hoffman Raises $24.6 Million for AI Cancer-Research Startup
item.description: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

item.title: Can an App Replace a Personal Trainer? I Tested Nearly a Dozen to Find Out
item.description: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.
